summary.intent: Implement a series of UI/UX improvements to the Gossamer Electron app: redacted thinking indicators, branch selector dropdown, commit message display in checkpoints, CheckpointRow redesign, URL-encoded search, and a two-panel SessionDetail/BranchLog layout with checkpoint sidebar.
summary.outcome: All features were implemented and builds passed clean. The two-panel layout was applied to both SessionDetail and BranchLog, with per-checkpoint event filtering, composite session:checkpoint selection keys, and a live 'In progress' shadow session item at the top of each panel.

repo: LogContent rows have a contentType field with values: text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, redacted_thinking — redacted_thinking blocks have thinking=null
CheckpointRow is defined in SessionDetail.tsx and imported by BranchLog.tsx — changes apply to both views from one definition
Git log format %H%x00%B%x1e captures both commit hash and body — parsed at the null byte boundary
Shadow sessions are linked to working branches via gitBranch field on ShadowSession, populated by indexer reading the first gitBranch value from JSONL log events

Each session gets its own shadow branch named entire/<base-commit[:7]>-<worktree-id-hash[:6]> — the association to a working branch is inferred from log events, not stored explicitly by the entire CLI
Multiple checkpoints can share the same checkpointId across different sessions — selection key must be composite sessionId:checkpointId to distinguish them
The /api/branch-log endpoint needed to be extended to include liveSession for the branch, queried via ShadowSession.gitBranch

code.finding: CheckpointRow renders commitMessage as monospace dimmed fallback when sum?.intent is absent
buildCheckpointGroups logic buckets events by timestamp comparison against checkpoint createdAt — events before first checkpoint go to that checkpoint, events after last go to shadow bucket
Comment explains same session appears across multiple shadow branches as transcript grows; each branch may have a different SHA for same underlying content — deduplication is by blob SHA
Live session lookup for a branch uses: repoDb.shadowSession.findFirst({ where: { gitBranch: branch } })

workflow: When lazy-loading state into a dropdown causes re-render that closes the dropdown, switching to eager load on mount (useEffect) fixes the interaction
Mantine Select dropdowns are sensitive to state changes during open — all data should be loaded before the user can interact with the control
TypeScript build (npm run build) is the fastest way to validate changes across the renderer and server without running the full app

friction: Branch dropdown was placed in two wrong locations (ActiveSessions, then Repos) before the correct location (Layout.tsx header, only on /branch-log route) was specified
Copy action was overriding the branch dropdown in Repos.tsx due to state update during open — required diagnosing re-render timing issue
Edit tool failed when old_string didn't match because a prior edit to the same file had already changed the surrounding context — required re-reading the file first
Multiple checkpoints sharing the same checkpointId across different sessions caused all to be selected together — required switching to composite sessionId:checkpointId key
open_items: Shadow session / live 'In progress' association relies on gitBranch being correctly written into JSONL events — if Claude Code emits it inconsistently or not at all, the branch association breaks

Checking out a new branch mid-session does not update ShadowSession.gitBranch — the association is locked to the branch active at session start; new branch work only appears after starting a new session

The relationship between shadow branches and working branches from the entire CLI side uses base commit hash, not branch name — the current server-side lookup by gitBranch may be fragile if the indexer misses events
